title: Place the System into Shutdown from the HMI
summary: The training states that after bag out the system returns to running, and that shutdown is a deliberate state the operator chooses from the Viva HMI when the next sort is not imminent.
when_to_use: Use when bag out has completed and the operator intends to place the system into shutdown rather than leave it in running state ready for another sort.
not_for: Do not use if you expect the system to enter shutdown automatically after bag out; the source states it returns to running unless shutdown is chosen.
Do not use as a detailed HMI navigation procedure; the source does not provide the exact screen path, button label, or confirmation sequence.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Use the Viva HMI to intentionally place the system into shutdown.

safety_notes: Use only the Viva HMI-based shutdown selection described by the source.
The source does not provide any lockout, physical isolation, or production-stop prerequisites.

access_required: Authority to select shutdown from the HMI
system_preconditions: Bag out has completed if this procedure is being used in the post-bag-out scenario described by the source.
The system is in running state unless shutdown has already been intentionally selected.

visual_references.description: Training video frame and aligned transcript discussing running versus shutdown state, shutdown queues, and that after bag out the system returns to running unless an operator uses the HMI to place the system into shutdown.
Overall system Aveva API page showing System State actions including System Startup, System Bag Out, System Shutdown, and System Purge.

steps.title: Confirm the system is in running after bag out
steps.purpose: Establish that bag out completion does not itself place the system into shutdown.
steps.instruction: Check the current system state and confirm that after bag out completion the system is in running state rather than assuming it is already in shutdown.
steps.expected_result: The operator confirms the system remains in running after bag out unless shutdown has been deliberately selected.
steps.healthy_condition: System state is running after bag out completion, consistent with the training description.
steps.failure_condition: System state is unclear, already shows shutdown unexpectedly, or does not align with the source-described post-bag-out behavior.

actions.label: Check current system state
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the system is in running after bag out completion rather than already in shutdown.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Transcript-supported distinction between bag out completion, running state, and shutdown as a separate operator-selected state.
Overall system page area showing System State functions including shutdown.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the current system state cannot be determined from the HMI.
Escalate if the system does not appear to return to running after bag out as described by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: “once it's done with bag out, it will go to a running state” and “unless you choose to put the system shut down”
After bag out, the system automatically returns to running and is ready for another sort; shutdown is a separate operator choice made afterward if needed.

steps.title: Select shutdown from the Viva HMI
steps.purpose: Intentionally place the system into shutdown using the operator interface identified by the source.
steps.instruction: Use the Viva HMI to choose shutdown. The source supports that shutdown is something the operator chooses from the HMI, but it does not provide the exact screen path, button label, or confirmation sequence.
steps.expected_result: A deliberate shutdown request is made from the Viva HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: Shutdown is selected through the HMI rather than inferred or expected to happen automatically.
steps.failure_condition: Shutdown cannot be selected from the HMI, or the operator cannot identify the correct control from the source-provided information.

actions.label: Choose shutdown
actions.exact_action: Use the Viva HMI to choose shutdown, based on the source statement that shutdown is selected from the HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall system page showing System Shutdown as one of the available system state actions.
Transcript evidence that shutdown is chosen from the HMI and references use of a Viva HMI.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if shutdown cannot be selected from the HMI as described.
Stop and escalate if the HMI path, button label, or confirmation sequence is required, because the source does not provide those details.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The speakers say shutdown is “something you choose from the HMI” and mention “using a Viva HMI” to put in a shutdown.
The overall system page shows System State actions including System Startup, System Bag Out, System Shutdown, and System Purge.
Training states that shutdown is selected by the operator from the HMI, rather than occurring automatically after bag out.

steps.title: Verify shutdown was intentionally entered
steps.purpose: Confirm the system entered shutdown because the operator selected it, not because of an incorrect assumption about bag out behavior.
steps.instruction: Verify that shutdown was intentionally entered rather than assumed to happen automatically after bag out.
steps.expected_result: The operator confirms shutdown is the result of an intentional HMI action.
steps.healthy_condition: System shutdown status is understood as a deliberate operator-selected state.
steps.failure_condition: There is uncertainty whether shutdown occurred intentionally, or personnel still believe shutdown should have happened automatically after bag out.

actions.label: Confirm intentional shutdown state
actions.exact_action: Verify that shutdown was intentionally entered rather than assumed to happen automatically after bag out.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Evidence that running follows bag out unless shutdown is separately chosen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the system state transition cannot be tied to an intentional HMI shutdown selection.
Escalate if operators are relying on automatic post-bag-out shutdown behavior not supported by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: After bag out the system goes to running state unless you choose to put the system shut down.
After bag out, the system automatically returns to running and is ready for another sort; shutdown is a separate operator choice made afterward if needed.
Shutdown is selected from the HMI.

steps.title: Observe shutdown queue and charging behavior
steps.purpose: Confirm the expected shutdown behavior described in the training after shutdown is chosen.
steps.instruction: Expect AGVs to go into shutdown logic and charging behavior after shutdown is chosen, consistent with the training description.
steps.expected_result: AGVs are associated with shutdown queues and charger rotation behavior consistent with shutdown state.
steps.healthy_condition: AGVs sit in shutdown queues when shut down and shutdown behavior includes AGVs rotating on chargers.
steps.failure_condition: AGVs do not appear to follow the described shutdown queue or charging behavior, or shutdown completion is not evident.

actions.label: Observe AGV shutdown behavior
actions.exact_action: Expect AGVs to go into shutdown logic and charging behavior after shutdown is chosen, consistent with the training description.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Transcript-supported references to shutdown queues as where AGVs sit when shut down.
Lifecycle description showing AGVs returning to shutdown and cycling back to chargers.
System Shutdown function and description that shutdown puts the system into a state for AGVs to rotate on chargers.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if AGVs do not appear to enter shutdown queue behavior after shutdown is selected.
Escalate if AGVs do not rotate on chargers as described by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: “Shut down queues is just where it sits when it's shut down... when they start up, they move into their own zones”
Shutdown puts the system into a state for AGVs to rotate on chargers.
AGVs return to shutdown with AGVs cycling back to chargers to prepare for the next sort.
Shutdown queues are the holding location for AGVs in shutdown state; on startup they move into assigned zones, and shutdown logic includes cycling AGVs through charging.

success_criteria: The system is intentionally placed into shutdown from the Viva HMI.
Shutdown is understood as a deliberate operator-selected state, not an automatic result of bag out.
Observed AGV behavior is consistent with shutdown queues and charger rotation described by the source.
healthy_conditions: After bag out, the system is in running until shutdown is chosen.
Shutdown can be selected from the Viva HMI.
AGVs sit in shutdown queues when shut down.
Shutdown behavior includes AGVs rotating on chargers.
failure_conditions: Shutdown cannot be selected from the HMI as described.
Operators assume shutdown occurs automatically after bag out.
The source-required HMI details needed to execute the action are unavailable because the source does not provide exact navigation or control names.
AGV shutdown queue or charging behavior does not match the training description.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if shutdown cannot be selected from the HMI as described by the source.
Escalate if the current system state cannot be confirmed as running after bag out or shutdown after selection.
Escalate if AGVs do not exhibit the shutdown queue or charger rotation behavior described in the training.
Escalate for SME review because the source does not provide the exact HMI screen path, button label, or confirmation sequence.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Running-versus-shutdown explanation, shutdown queues, and post-bag-out behavior.
Overall system page showing System Shutdown among system state actions.
Lifecycle view showing shutdown and AGV charger cycling behavior.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The speakers say shutdown is “something you choose from the HMI” and mention “using a Viva HMI” to put in a shutdown.

missing_details: The source does not provide the exact Viva HMI screen path for selecting shutdown.
The source does not provide the exact shutdown button label or control name.
The source does not provide the confirmation sequence or expected on-screen confirmation text after selecting shutdown.
The source does not provide a time estimate for shutdown completion.
The source does not define explicit fault codes or thresholds for failed shutdown.
